# meshstack_building_block_runner (Resource)

Manages a meshBuildingBlockRunner in meshStack. Building block runners are agents that execute building block runs. 

~> **Preview:** This resource is in preview. Breaking changes are possible without prior notice due to changes in the underlying [meshStack preview API](https://docs.meshcloud.io/api/technical-specifications#preview-endpoints) or due to changes in this provider. Please ensure you are running the latest version of the provider and report any bugs via [GitHub issues](https://github.com/meshcloud/terraform-provider-meshstack/issues) or via support@meshcloud.io.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "meshstack_building_block_runner" "example" {
  metadata = {
    owned_by_workspace = data.meshstack_workspace.example.metadata.name
  }

  spec = {
    display_name        = "My Terraform Runner"
    implementation_type = "TERRAFORM"
    public_key          = "-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\nMIICIjANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQEFAAOCAg8AMIICCgKCAgEA...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----"
    restriction         = "PRIVATE"
  }
}
```

```terraform
resource "meshstack_building_block_runner" "example_with_wif" {
  metadata = {
    owned_by_workspace = data.meshstack_workspace.example.metadata.name
  }

  spec = {
    display_name        = "My GCP WIF Runner"
    implementation_type = "TERRAFORM"
    public_key          = "-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\nMIICIjANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQEFAAOCAg8AMIICCgKCAgEA...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----"
    restriction         = "PRIVATE"

    workload_identity_federation = {
      subject = "system:serviceaccount:namespace:my-runner"
      issuer  = "https://oidc.example.com"

      gcp = {
        audience   = "gcp-workload-identity-provider:namespace"
        token_path = "/var/run/secrets/workload-identity/token"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `metadata` (Attributes) Metadata of the building block runner. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--metadata))
- `spec` (Attributes) Specification of the building block runner. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--spec))

### Read-Only

- `ref` (Attributes) Reference to this runner, can be used in building block definitions. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ref))

<a id="nestedatt--metadata"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metadata`

Required:

- `owned_by_workspace` (String) Identifier of the workspace that owns this runner.

Read-Only:

- `created_on` (String) Timestamp when the runner was created (ISO 8601).
- `last_seen` (String) Timestamp when the runner last connected to meshStack (ISO 8601).
- `uuid` (String) Unique identifier of the runner. Assigned by meshStack on creation.


<a id="nestedatt--spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec`

Required:

- `display_name` (String) Human-readable display name of the runner.
- `implementation_type` (String) Type of building block implementation this runner handles. One of: `TERRAFORM`, `GITHUB_WORKFLOW`, `GITLAB_PIPELINE`, `AZURE_DEVOPS_PIPELINE`, `MANUAL`, `ALL`. Use `ALL` to handle building blocks of any implementation type.
- `public_key` (String) RSA public key in PEM format (`BEGIN PUBLIC KEY`) or an X.509 certificate (`BEGIN CERTIFICATE`). meshStack uses this key to encrypt secrets sent to the runner.

Optional:

- `restriction` (String) Visibility restriction of the runner. `PUBLIC` makes the runner available to all workspaces. `PRIVATE` (default) restricts it to the owning workspace. Only administrators can set `PUBLIC`.
- `workload_identity_federation` (Attributes) Optional workload identity federation configuration. When provided, at least one of `gcp`, `aws`, or `azure` must be set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--spec--workload_identity_federation))

Read-Only:

- `is_self_hosted` (Boolean) Indicates whether the runner is self-hosted. This field is read-only and set by meshStack.

<a id="nestedatt--spec--workload_identity_federation"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.workload_identity_federation`

Required:

- `issuer` (String) The OIDC issuer URL of the identity provider that issues tokens for this runner. This is used to configure trust with the target cloud provider.
- `subject` (String) The subject claim of the OIDC token issued to this runner, e.g., `system:serviceaccount:namespace:my-runner`.

Optional:

- `aws` (Attributes) AWS workload identity federation configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--spec--workload_identity_federation--aws))
- `azure` (Attributes) Azure workload identity federation configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--spec--workload_identity_federation--azure))
- `gcp` (Attributes) GCP workload identity federation configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--spec--workload_identity_federation--gcp))

<a id="nestedatt--spec--workload_identity_federation--aws"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.workload_identity_federation.aws`

Required:

- `audience` (String) Audience value for the federated identity token.
- `token_path` (String) Path to the federated identity token file on the runner.


<a id="nestedatt--spec--workload_identity_federation--azure"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.workload_identity_federation.azure`

Required:

- `audience` (String) Audience value for the federated identity token.
- `token_path` (String) Path to the federated identity token file on the runner.


<a id="nestedatt--spec--workload_identity_federation--gcp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.workload_identity_federation.gcp`

Required:

- `audience` (String) Audience value for the federated identity token.
- `token_path` (String) Path to the federated identity token file on the runner.




<a id="nestedatt--ref"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ref`

Read-Only:

- `kind` (String) meshObject type, always `meshBuildingBlockRunner`.
- `uuid` (String) UUID (`metadata.uuid`) of `meshBuildingBlockRunner`.
